title: Allowing shell scripts execution during package installation is security-sensitive
description: Allowing shell scripts execution during package installation is security-sensitiveAnalyze your coderesponsibility-trustworthysecuritySecurity HotspotcweWhen installing dependencies, package managers likenpmwill automatically execute shell scripts distributed along with the source code. Post-install scripts, for example, are a common way to execute malicious code at install time whenever a package is compromised.Ask Yourself WhetherThe execution of dependency installation scripts is required for the application to function correctly.There is a risk if you answered no to the question.Recommended Secure Coding PracticesExecution of third-party scripts should be disabled if not strictly necessary for dependencies to work correctly. Doing this will reduce the attack surface and block a well-kno
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Package installation without --ignore-scripts flag is security-sensitive. Package managers automatically execute shell scripts during installation, which can be exploited if a dependency is compromised. Use --ignore-scripts flag to disable automatic script execution (e.g., 'npm install --ignore-scripts').
